macro_segments.title: Salt provision for priests' sacrifices and communal funds

micro_segments.title: Introduction to the daf and salting practices in the Temple
Ravina's question to Rav Ashi: clarifying the stipulation for priest salt use
Baraita: who provides salt for sacrifices—comparison to frankincense and wood
Gemara's logical derivation: salt provided by communal funds like wood
Two interpretive approaches: salt as קומץ component vs. general offering supply
Rav Mordechai's explanation: Ben Buchri and priest exemption from communal funds
Ben Buchri's testimony: priests not obligated to contribute shekalim
Rabban Yochanan ben Zakkai's counter-position: priests must contribute
Priests' interpretation of the מנחת כהן verse to avoid contribution
Implications and contemporary parallels: entitlement vs. reciprocal obligation

macro_segments.title: Wood and fire provision by communal funds: comparison to altar

micro_segments.title: Baraita: wood and fire must be provided by communal funds, not individually
Rabbi Elazar ben Shamua: wood and fire must not be used by laypeople
Gemara analysis: difference between communal provision vs. newness requirement
Biblical example of threshing implements as wood: definition of morag

macro_segments.title: Mixing of קומץ portions from multiple מנחות and פיגול principles

micro_segments.title: Mishna: קומץ mixed with other fully-burned מנחות is כשר
Rabbi Yehuda: mixture with thick vs. thin dough is invalid

macro_segments.title: Principle of ביטול (nullification) in blood and קרבנות

micro_segments.title: Mishna from Zevachim: blood mixed with water—appearance determines validity
Blood mixed with wine: treating wine as water for appearance test
Blood mixed with non-sacrifice blood: treating other blood as water
Debate: Rabbi Yehuda holds blood does not nullify blood
Gemara: both derive from same verse—ox and goat blood on Yom Kippur
Rabbis' position: עולים (offerings) do not nullify each other
Rabbi Yehuda's position: like does not nullify like (מין במין)
Gemara's difficulty: which feature (עולים or מין במין) determines the halakha
Tosafot's resolution: nullification rules differ for לח בלח vs. יבש ביבש

macro_segments.title: Application of nullification principle to mixed מנחה doughs with differing oil ratios

micro_segments.title: Gemara's question: why does mixture of similar items create nullification problem
Rava's answer: flour from one mencha nullifies absorbed oil from the other
Unresolved tension: how Sages disagree on whether nullification applies here

topical_tags.term: Ben Buchri vs. Rabban Yochanan ben Zakkai
Fresh vs. used materials (חדתא vs. עתיקי)
Offerings do not nullify each other (אין עולים מבטלים)
Like does not nullify like (מין במין לא בטיל)
Thick vs. thin mixture (עבה vs. רכה)
Liquid vs. solid nullification (לח בלח vs. יבש ביבש)
Mencha of anointed priest (מנחת כהן משיח)
